compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Why

Mutation testing currently covers only five behavioral cores (signing, gates,
next-batch, validate, reducer processors) per `docs/mutation-testing.md`; the concurrency
machinery the other stories harden — sync branch selection, compaction fold/rollback
decisions, push policy/retry classification, lock stale-owner/reclamation — has no
mutation evidence. Once behavioral tests for those paths exist (S1, S3, S4), narrow
mutation shards over their pure decision logic will confirm the new assertions actually
kill mutants rather than just raising line coverage.

## What

- Extract small pure decision helpers where needed and add narrow mutation-testing
  shards for them, rather than mutating whole subprocess-heavy orchestration modules.

## Scope

- `docs/mutation-testing.md` (shard config / `only_mutate` additions)
- Small helper extractions in `src/rebar/_store/{sync,push,lock}.py` and
  `src/rebar/_commands/compact.py` decision logic, if needed for tractable mutation.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Mutation shards target the paths that have behavioral tests once their prerequisite
      stories land: compaction fold/rollback (S1), remote-append (S4), push (S3). Sync
      branch selection and lock stale-owner/reclamation are only sharded if this story
      also adds their behavioral tests; otherwise they are explicitly DESCOPED here and
      noted as requiring a follow-up story before mutation shards are meaningful.
- [ ] Shards target pure/near-pure helpers (extracted if necessary) — not whole
      subprocess-heavy modules — to avoid no-test attribution and equivalent-mutant noise.
- [ ] Mapped scores for the new shards are recorded in `docs/mutation-testing.md`.
- [ ] This story is scheduled AFTER S1/S3/S4 land (documented as depends_on).
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` pass.
compiled_state.title: Selective mutation-testing expansion for concurrency decision logic
